summary: I read both mandatory guides (`guides/backend-guide.md`, `guides/ui-style-guide.md`) and the three Define-phase artifacts under review — `requirements.md`, `discovery_notes.md`, and `open_questions.md` — then checked the design/specs against the guides' service/Result and thin-controller rules, query-object placement, model/callback rules, Tailwind type scale and semantic status colors, Turbo/live-update conventions, and testing expectations. The requirements are intentionally behavior-level and impose no choices that conflict with either guide (and reinforce the "status never by color alone" a11y rule via R12/R35), while the discovery notes correctly route aggregate reads into `app/queries/`, keep the controller thin, mandate broadcasts from services after commit, and reuse existing status-badge/card/empty-state/type-scale conventions and `StepRuns::Sweep` fleet constants. The only real-time tension (heartbeats via Turbo Streams vs. Q9's proposed periodic refresh) is explicitly surfaced as an open question for human decision rather than silently drifting, which is the guide-correct handling. I emitted a `pass` verdict with no findings to `.pipeliner/phases/01-define/main/guide-alignment-critic/verdict.json`.
